content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 907 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

20 April 1977

Gordon J. Tucker
Apr. #9
1034 Sutter Street
San Francisco, CA 94109

Dear Mr. Tucker:

Thank you for your letter of April 12, 1977. I apologize for not replying sooner to your earlier letter.

The filing requirements of the Political Reform Act, Chapter 7, Article 2, do not require that a notation be made, such as "none" on schedules that do not apply to the filer. Likewise, the Instruction Manual for Statements of Economic Interests does not require the filer to indicate that no interests are held for particular schedules.

If you have any further questions, please contact this office.

Sincerely,

Dawn Wiser

April 12, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California    95814

Attn: Mr. Daniel Lowenstein


Enclosed is a copy of my January 24, 1977 letter to you.

You received the letter because I subsequently received the Economic
Interest Statements requested in paragraph #2. Also, I received your
invoice for the service and have since paid it in full.

However, I have not received any reply to paragraph #4 (as marked) of
the letter and regarding Assemblyman Willie Browns Economic Interests
Report which you sent me earlier.

Will you please reply to my questions in paragraph #4 at your earliest
convenience.

Thank you,

Gordon J. Tucker

encl: ltr-1-24-77 to F.P.P.C.


Apt.#9
1034 Sutter Street
San Francisco, California
                94109

January 24 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814


Enclosed is my personal check # 13 of Jan. 24, 1977 in amount $12.70 to
pay your invoice dated Jan. 17, 1977.

Please mail me copies of Economic Interests statements filed by the
following Senators:
                    Ayala, R.S.
                    Deukmejian, Geo.
                    Dunlap, J.F.
                    Holmdahl, J.W.
                    Nimmo, R.
                    Petris, N.C.
                    Richardson, H.L.
                    Roberti, D.A.
                    Song, A.H.

Please mail me the invoice for these copies to the below address.

Also, referring to the copy of the Economic Interests Report you sent me
as filed by Assemblyman Willie Brown--Form 721-C and 721-E and 721-G
are not completed in any manner, i.e., no statement or notation has been
made by the Assemblyman on the Schedule Forms stating that they apply to him
or not. Other Assembly members make notes such as "none", etc. to show
that they saw the form(s) and acknowledge a responsibility to reply to them on
a positive basis. What requirements in this respect are mandated by Chapter
7, Article 2, of the Political Reform Act of 1974 and by the "Instruction
Manual for Statement of Economic Interest-Form 721" ?

Thank you for your assistance.

Gordon J. Tucker

Box 9
1034 Sutter Street
San Francisco, Ca. 94109

encl: Ck# 13, inv copy.
